commit.message:
Fix stale executions permanently blocking concurrency=forbid jobs (#1947)

* Initial plan

* Fix stale execution cleanup for concurrency=forbid jobs

When a running execution gets stuck in persistent storage (e.g., due to
node crash or gRPC failure), it permanently blocks jobs with
concurrency=forbid from running.

The fix cross-references storage-based running executions with in-memory
active executions. If an execution is in storage but not active in memory
and has been running longer than DefaultStaleExecutionThreshold (4 hours),
it is automatically cleaned up by marking it as failed via Raft.

Co-authored-by: vcastellm <47026+vcastellm@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: use UTC times consistently, improve error logging

Co-authored-by: vcastellm <47026+vcastellm@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Update about section text on homepage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add scroll-margin-top for sticky header deep-link offset

section[id] and div[id] now receive scroll-margin-top: 4.5rem so that
hash-link navigation (#cv-packages, #why-nexus, #job-search etc.) scrolls
the target into view below the fixed site header rather than hiding it.

The smooth scroll behaviour was already declared in this file; this
completes it with the offset needed for sticky-nav pages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add id=cv-packages to section element for stable deep link anchor

The cv-packages.html include now exposes id="cv-packages" directly on
the <section> element. Previously only the wrapper div in find-a-job
had this id; the inner section was anonymous, breaking deep links that
target the section's own boundary.

Anchor /job-seekers/find-a-job#cv-packages now scrolls to the section
with correct sticky-header offset applied by the scroll-margin-top rule
added in src_style.css.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: deep link anchors, scroll offset, and rebuild CSS

- Add id="cv-packages" to <section> in cv-packages.html so the include
  exposes a stable anchor matching the wrapper div in find-a-job/index.html;
  links of the form /job-seekers/find-a-job#cv-packages now resolve to the
  correct section element.
- Add scroll-margin-top (4.5 rem) for section[id] and div[id] in
  src_style.css so that deep-link navigation accounts for the sticky
  site header and does not obscure content behind the navbar.
- Rebuild nexus_web.css (Tailwind v4 production build) to pick up the
  new scroll-margin-top utility generated from src_style.css.

Branding (already committed on this branch):
  - desk_theme.css: full light/dark navbar, sidebar, dropdown, and
    awesomebar contrast rules; .navbar-home .app-logo sizing.
  - nexus_web.js: MutationObserver-based logo swap (LOGO_LIGHT/LOGO_DARK)
    keyed on data-theme attribute for correct contrast in both modes.
  - hooks.py: app_logo_url = /assets/nexus_web/logo.svg (correct).
  - logo-dark.svg: dark-mode variant asset added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- ExecuteExtractSubAsync: 新增 MAX_SUBTITLE_ENTRIES=50000 和 MAX_JSON_BYTES=16MB 上限，超出时记录 WARN 日志并提前 break，防止超大字幕文件耗尽内存

commit.message:
优化: 字幕默认选择改为优先内嵌字幕，再回退外置字幕

原逻辑：优先外置字幕（外置 → 内嵌兜底 index=0）
新逻辑：优先第一条内嵌字幕 → 无内嵌时选第一条外置字幕 → 都无则不显示

findPreferredTrackIndex() 在基类 SubtitleBridgeAdapterBase，
AVPlayer 和 ijkplayer 路径均继承该方法，一次修改两路同时生效。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

优化: 内嵌字幕轨道去掉"内嵌字幕 N"前缀，直接显示语言描述信息

外部字幕已有 [外部·SRT] 标识，内嵌字幕去掉冗余前缀后可展示更多有效信息。
- IjkSubtitleBridgeAdapter：直接使用 buildSubtitleTrackDisplayName() 返回值
  如 "中文 (ASS) 简体"、"英语 (SRT)"
- AvSubtitleBridgeAdapter：直接使用语言名称，无语言时退回 "字幕 N"

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

修复: 内嵌字幕轨道列表显示恢复简体/繁体标注

IjkSubtitleBridgeAdapter.loadInternalTracks() 改用
FfprobeUtil.buildSubtitleTrackDisplayName()，
该方法通过 FfprobeTrack.title 调用 getSubtitleTier3Label() 区分简体/繁体。
示例："内嵌字幕 1 (中文 (ASS) 简体)" 而非仅显示 "内嵌字幕 1 (中文)"

commit.message:
修复: 内嵌字幕 ASS 解析 — 检测 Dialogue 冒号格式，跳 9 逗号提取 Text 字段

根因：rect->ass 是含乱码'Dialogue'单词的完整行格式：
  <garbled>: Layer,Start,End,Style,Name,0,0,0,Effect,Text
上一版本检测第一字段是否为数字，无法匹配此格式，导致原始 ASS 元数据字符串透传显示。

修复逻辑（parseAssIfNeeded）：
1. 优先查找 ': '（冒号+空格）→ 跳过 9 个逗号到 Text 字段
2. 回退：第一字段为纯数字（MKV 包格式）→ 跳 8 个逗号
3. 无法识别结构（清除信号/垃圾包）→ 返回空字符串清除字幕

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(theming): Full Skins & Themes Implementation (#369) (#528)

* feat(theming): Full Skins & Themes implementation (#369)

Wave 1 - Core Theme Fidelity:
- ThemeMode enum (StyleSheetTheme/Theme) with dual-mode ApplyThemeSkin
- Sub-component style theming (SubStyles on ControlSkin, SkinBuilder.SubStyle())
- 5 data controls override ApplyThemeSkin: GridView, DetailsView, FormView, DataGrid, DataList
- Container-level EnableTheming propagation via ancestor chain walk
- Runtime theme switching via ThemeProvider Mode parameter
- Fix generic type name lookup (GridView1 -> GridView) for theme skin matching

Wave 2 - Migration Accelerators:
- .skin file parser (SkinFileParser) - reads Web Forms .skin files into ThemeConfiguration
- JSON theme format (JsonThemeLoader) - load/save themes as JSON with custom converters
- CSS file bundling - ThemeProvider renders <link> elements via HeadContent

Wave 3 - Diagnostics:
- ThemeDiagnostics with validation rules for unknown controls, sub-styles, empty skins
- Runtime SkinID mismatch logging in BaseWebFormsComponent

Tests: 120 theming tests (72 Wave 1 + 48 Wave 2), 2685 total tests passing
Docs: themes-and-skins.md with migration guide, API reference, quick start

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Migration is now: copy App_Themes/ to wwwroot/App_Themes/ — zero extra lines needed.

commit.message:
pdf-object: implement ASCII85Decode filter

Add `Filter::ASCII85Decode` variant, map the `"ASCII85Decode"` name in
the `From` impl, and implement `Filter::decode_ascii85` using Horner's
method with wrapping arithmetic. Wire it into `StreamObject::data()`.

Includes 7 unit tests covering full groups, the `z` shorthand, partial
final groups, whitespace skipping, the `~>` EOD marker, and invalid
character errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: exclude cost-optimization from policy sync to fix validation

The Datree CRD catalog schema for kyverno.io/clusterpolicy_v1 rejects
the configMap.key field with additionalProperties: false, causing
kubeconform to fail on the synced schedule-based-quotas sample. Since
this is a schema bug and the sample is not deployed, exclude the
cost-optimization category from sync and remove the existing file.

* ci: re-trigger after adding SOPS_AGE_KEY to ci environment

* ci: skip system test in merge queue

commit.message:
Add 12 platform-specific IPC examples (#64)

* Add file-watcher example with inotify-backed server and tests

Linux directory watcher using inotify that demonstrates IPC notifications.
Server watches directories and broadcasts FileChanged events to clients.
Seven integration tests cover watch/unwatch lifecycle, event notifications
for file creation/modification/deletion, and error handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add sysmon example with /proc-based CPU, memory, and load monitoring

Server reads /proc/stat, /proc/meminfo, and /proc/loadavg to implement
GetCpuUsage, GetMemoryInfo, and GetLoadAverage RPCs. A monitoring thread
checks thresholds every second and fires HighCpuAlert/LowMemoryAlert
notifications.

Client queries all three methods in a formatted table and listens for
threshold notifications. Test suite covers all RPCs, both notification
types, invalid method handling, and RunLoop dispatch mode (8 tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add disk-usage example with simulated volumes

Implement the disk-usage example under examples/macos/disk-usage/:

- disk_device.cpp: Simulated server with 3 volumes (/, /home, /tmp).
  Usage on / grows 1 GB every 3s; fires DiskSpaceLow when > 90%.
- disk_client.cpp: Interactive CLI with list, check <mount>, quit
  commands and bar-chart display. Prints notification alerts.
- disk_test.cpp: 8 Google Test cases covering GetVolumeCount,
  GetVolumeAt (valid/invalid), GetDiskUsage (known/unknown mount),
  DiskSpaceLow notification, UsedPercent consistency, RunLoop dispatch.
- CMakeLists.txt: Builds device, client, and test executables.
- README.md: IDL overview, build/run instructions, sample output.

All volumes are simulated — no platform-specific APIs — so the example
compiles and runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add audio-dsp example: simulated DSP effects processor

Implement server (dsp_device), client CLI (dsp_dashboard), and 12 Google
Test cases covering slot management, parameter updates, effect counting,
signal levels, invalid-slot errors, and ClipDetected/LevelUpdate
notifications.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add can-bus-ecu example: simulated ECU with OBD-II PIDs

Implement ecu_device (server with state machine simulation),
ecu_dashboard (interactive CLI client), and ecu_test (11 Google
Test cases covering all RPC methods, notifications, and RunLoop
dispatch). Cross-platform — compiles on Linux and Windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add BLE Heart Rate Monitor peripheral example

Implement simulated BLE HRM with GATT service model:
- ble_device: server with Heart Rate (0x180D) and Battery (0x180F)
  services, simulation thread for HR/battery updates
- ble_dashboard: interactive CLI client with service discovery,
  characteristic read, and notification subscription
- ble_test: 9 Google Test cases covering service count, get/read/
  write characteristics, notification enable/disable, error cases

All code is cross-platform (no macOS-specific APIs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add motor-controller example with simulated stepper motor

Implements MotorController IPC service with trapezoidal motion profiles,
limit switch detection, stall monitoring, and homing sequence. Includes:

- motor_device: simulated stepper motor server (10ms tick loop)
- motor_dashboard: interactive CLI client with status display
- motor_test: 10 Google Test cases covering all methods and notifications

All code is cross-platform (no Windows-specific APIs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CMake wiring and build warnings for platform examples

- Consolidate platform subdirectory includes in examples/CMakeLists.txt
- Guard Linux-only examples (sysmon, file-watcher) inside linux/CMakeLists.txt
- Remove duplicate add_subdirectory entries from parallel agent commits
- Fix -Werror=unused-result warnings in filewatcher_test.cpp

* Address code review findings across platform examples

- Fix dead code and wrong formula in battery_device timeToEmpty calc
- Move notifyXxx calls outside mutex locks in motor, gpio, procmon, svcstat
- Add can-bus-ecu to windows/CMakeLists.txt (was missing)
- Replace fragile reference capture in ble_device with member m_simRunning
- Add explicit null termination after strncpy in disk_device
- Move ecu_device printf inside lock scope to avoid data race

* Fix motor simulation crossover on coarse-timer platforms

On Windows (~15ms timer resolution) and macOS, a single simulation
tick can overshoot the MoveTo target position. Add post-update
crossover detection in both the production device and test device
to snap to target when position crosses or reaches the destination.

Fixes CI failures: MoveTo_ReachesTarget, MoveWithoutHome_Allowed

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cli): raise click.UsageError for reversed --since/--until range (#454)

Move reversed date-range validation from _filter_sessions (report layer)
to the summary and cost CLI commands. This replaces the raw Python
UserWarning with a proper click.UsageError that exits with code 2 and
a human-readable message.

- cli.py: Add since > until check in summary() and cost() before
  calling render functions
- report.py: Remove warnings.warn from _filter_sessions; silently
  return [] for reversed ranges
- test_cli.py: Add CliRunner tests for both summary and cost with
  reversed range
- test_report.py: Update existing tests to assert no warning is emitted
- test_e2e.py: Update e2e test to expect non-zero exit instead of
  UserWarning

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: add weekly scheduled run for dependency drift detection (#126)

Add a schedule trigger to ci.yml (Mondays 06:00 UTC) so cargo audit
and npm audit run automatically between releases, catching new CVEs
before the next PR. cancel-in-progress is disabled for scheduled runs
so alerts are never silently dropped.

Document the weekly audit cadence in CONTRIBUTING.md so contributors
know they do not need to run manual audits.

Closes #116

commit.message:
feat: add URL deep linking, keyboard search navigation, and favicon to syu app (#132)

- Hash-based URL routing: navigation state persisted in window.location.hash
  (#section/item-id format). On load, restores state from hash. jumpToItem
  and selectSection update the hash via history.replaceState so items are
  directly shareable via URL.

- Keyboard navigation in search: ArrowDown/Up moves through results,
  Enter selects focused or sole result, Escape clears query. Focused
  result highlighted with ring-2 ring-sky-400. Added role=combobox,
  aria-expanded, aria-controls ARIA attributes.

- Item chip label: 'Items in this document' label added above pill row.
  Each pill button now has title attribute showing the full item title.

- Favicon: add app/public/favicon.svg and link it in index.html.

Closes #122

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add onboarding banner, trace tooltips, and back navigation to syu app (#130)

* feat: add onboarding banner, trace tooltips, and back navigation to syu app

Add three UX improvements for first-time users:

1. Dismissible welcome banner at the top of the main content area
   explaining the four-layer model. Dismissal is persisted in
   localStorage so the banner only shows once per browser.

2. Trace panel tooltips: SYMBOLS and DOC CONTAINS now have ⓘ title
   attributes explaining what each field verifies. Empty symbols
   display as 'any symbol (wildcard)'; empty doc_contains displays
   as 'not declared' instead of bare '—'.

3. Navigation history back button: jumpToItem() pushes the previous
   item to a history stack (capped at 5). A '← Back' button appears
   in the detail header when history is available.

Closes #121

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* app: address onboarding review feedback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add Linux ARM64 (aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu) release target (#127)

* ci: add Linux ARM64 (aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu) release target

Add aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu to the release-artifacts matrix using
cross-compilation from ubuntu-latest with gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu.
Add .cargo/config.toml linker entry for the cross target.

The installer script already detects aarch64 on Linux and resolves
the correct target triple, so no installer changes are needed.

Closes #115

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add alpha-to-next migration guide and contribution process (#129)

* docs: add alpha-to-next migration guide and contribution process

Create docs/guide/migration.md with per-release upgrade notes covering:
- v0.0.1-alpha.7: require_reciprocal_links now on by default, report.output added
- v0.0.1-alpha.6: spec.root default changed from docs/spec to docs/syu, new SYU-* rule code format
- v0.0.1-alpha.5: hierarchical folder support, runtime auto-detection

Add 'Migration notes' section to CONTRIBUTING.md requiring PRs with
breaking changes to update migration.md before merge.

Link to migration.md from README.md Release automation section.

Closes #119

commit.message:
docs: add troubleshooting guide for syu validate errors (#128)

* docs: add troubleshooting guide for syu validate errors

Create docs/guide/troubleshooting.md with plain-English explanations
for every validation error code, grouped by category (workspace,
graph, delivery, trace, coverage). Each entry includes the error
meaning, a minimal YAML example that triggers it, and a step-by-step
fix. A dedicated section explains when --fix is safe and lists common
false-confidence patterns.

Link to the guide from docs/guide/getting-started.md and README.md.

Closes #117

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: correct troubleshooting guide examples

commit.message:
refactor: split build_session_summary into focused helpers (#224)

Extract the ~200-line build_session_summary function into 4 focused
private helpers:

- _first_pass(): single pass over events to extract identity, shutdown
  data, and counters
- _detect_resume(): scan post-shutdown events for resume indicators
- _build_completed_summary(): merge shutdown cycles into SessionSummary
- _build_active_summary(): model inference + config fallback for active
  sessions

Two frozen dataclasses (_FirstPassResult, _ResumeInfo) carry state
between phases. The public API (signature + return type) is unchanged.

Closes #224

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve CI failure — add test for negative shutdown_model_calls guard

The diff-cover check failed because line 597 of report.py (the
defensive clamp of shutdown_model_calls to 0 when active_model_calls
exceeds model_calls) was not covered by tests.

Added test_negative_shutdown_model_calls_clamped_to_zero to cover
this edge case, bringing diff coverage to 100%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: show shutdown-only model calls on per-model cost rows (#409)

In render_cost_view, the per-model row displayed s.model_calls (total
across all periods) while all other columns used shutdown-only data from
s.model_metrics. For resumed sessions this caused the visual sum of
per-model + ↳ rows to exceed the grand total.

Subtract s.active_model_calls so the per-model row is consistent with
the other shutdown-only columns. For non-resumed sessions
active_model_calls is 0, so behaviour is unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: enable doctest execution for format_tokens/format_duration (#352)

Restore doctest examples in _formatting.py and enable --doctest-modules
in pyproject.toml so pytest collects and verifies them. Added boundary
test (format_duration(60000) -> '1m'). 9 doctest examples now run as
part of the normal test suite.

Closes #352
